prompt.content:
---
module: prompts
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_frontmatter, schemas_line_range, targets]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: the full-generation task names the module and embeds the spec
    given: { module: greeting, body: "Greet a person by name." }
    expect: { contains: ["implementing the module \"greeting\"", "Greet a person by name."], not_contains: ["INCREMENTAL update"] }
  - name: a prior failure appends the failure block
    given: { failure: "FAIL boom" }
    expect: { contains: ["A previous attempt FAILED its tests.", "FAIL boom"] }
  - name: the incremental task carries the diff and the intersecting region
    given: { region: "greet.ts lines 1-1 note 'builds greeting'" }
    expect: { contains: ["INCREMENTAL update", "-Greet a person by name.", "+Greet a person by name, loudly.", "greet.ts lines 1-1 — builds greeting"] }
  - name: the reconcile task carries the prompt file, body, and per-file diff
    given: { prompt_file: "examples/greeting.napl", file: ".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts" }
    expect: { contains: ["reconciling hand edits", "Prompt file to amend: examples/greeting.napl", "=== FILE: .napl/src/typescript/greet.ts ===", "+HELLO", "NEVER touch the generated source"] }
  - name: the change-required retry appends the critical escalation
    given: { base: "BASE" }
    expect: { starts_with: "BASE", contains: ["CRITICAL: A previous attempt made NO source changes"] }
  - name: the derivation system prompts carry their routing markers
    given: {}
    expect: { ir_contains: "intermediate representation", ml_contains: "MACHINE LAYER", attribution_has_neither: true }
  - name: the machine-layer user prompt marks NO CHANGES and empty output
    given: { change_summary: "NO CHANGES", agent_output: "" }
    expect: { contains: ["NO CHANGES", "(no output captured)"] }
---
# Coding-agent task and derivation-prompt builders

This module builds the exact prompt strings the toolchain feeds to coding agents
and to the IR / attribution / machine-layer derivation LLMs. **The exact text is
load-bearing**: the conformance corpus asserts substrings of these strings, and a
fake backend routes on markers inside the system prompts, so reproduce every
literal byte, line break, and constant precisely. It is pure: no I/O — every
function assembles and returns a `String` (or is a `&'static str` constant).

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `prompts/`: `prompts/Cargo.toml` (package name
`prompts`) and `prompts/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `prompts/`. Ensure
`cargo test` passes from the workspace root before finishing.

## Builds on four modules of this workspace

Each is a sibling member crate generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external
crates.io dependencies), use their public API, and do **not** reimplement them or
depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_frontmatter`** (`../schemas_frontmatter`): `Frontmatter` with public
  fields `module: String`, `deps: Vec<String>`, `targets: Vec<String>`, `tests`.
  The task builders read `module` and `deps`.
- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): `LineRange` with public
  `start`/`end` fields (`u32`).
- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): `AttributionEntry`
  (`Clone`) with public `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`,
  `lines: LineRange`, `note: String`.
- **`targets`** (`../targets`): `TargetAdapter` with a public
  `idiom_guidance: String` and a public `test_command_label: &'static str`, and
  the function `get_adapter(name: &str) -> Result<TargetAdapter, String>`. The
  task builders read `idiom_guidance` and `test_command_label` from the adapter
  they are given.

## The types

Expose these public types:

- `DepSummary` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`): public `module: String` and
  `summary: String`.
- `IncrementalTaskInput` (`Debug`, `Clone`): public `module: String`,
  `diff: String`, `intersecting_entries: Vec<AttributionEntry>`, and
  `owned_files: Vec<String>`.
- `ReconcileFile` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`): public `file: String` and
  `diff: String`.

## A shared truncation helper

`last_chars(text, count)` returns the last `count` **characters** (Unicode
scalar values, not bytes) of `text`; if `text` has `count` or fewer characters it
returns the whole `text`. Used to cap failure output at 6000 and agent output at
4000 characters below.

## The full-generation task

`build_agent_task(adapter: &TargetAdapter, frontmatter: &Frontmatter, body: &str, deps: &[DepSummary], failure: Option<&str>) -> String`
joins the following lines with `\n` (a bare bullet like `(empty)` denotes an
empty line):

1. `You are a coding agent implementing the module "{frontmatter.module}" as real, runnable`
2. `source code in the current working directory. Write and edit files directly; scaffold the`
3. `project and install dependencies as needed. The prompt below is the durable source of truth.`
4. (empty)
5. `Target idiom guidance:`
6. `{adapter.idiom_guidance}`
7. (empty)
8. `Module: {frontmatter.module}`
9. Only if `frontmatter.deps` is non-empty: `Declared dependencies: {frontmatter.deps joined by ", "}`
10. Only if `deps` is non-empty, three-plus lines: (empty), then `Other modules in this project (for context, do not reimplement them):`, then one `- {dep.module}: {dep.summary}` per `DepSummary`.
11. (empty)
12. `Specification to implement (implement exactly this behavior):`
13. `"""`
14. `{body.trim()}`
15. `"""`
16. (empty)
17. `Requirements:`
18. `- Ensure "{adapter.test_command_label}" passes from this directory before you finish.`
19. `- Write a real test suite covering the described behavior.`
20. `- Use only functions/hooks, named exports, and no dead code.`
21. `- Do not edit files under node_modules; use the package manager for dependencies.`
22. Only if `failure` is `Some(f)`, the **failure block**: (empty), then `A previous attempt FAILED its tests. Read the current files, fix the code and/or tests,`, then `and make the suite pass. Test output from the failed attempt:`, then a line of three backticks, then `{last_chars(f.trim(), 6000)}`, then a line of three backticks.

## The incremental task

`build_incremental_task(adapter: &TargetAdapter, frontmatter: &Frontmatter, body: &str, input: &IncrementalTaskInput, failure: Option<&str>) -> String`
joins with `\n`:

1. `You are a coding agent making an INCREMENTAL update to the existing module "{frontmatter.module}"`
2. `in the current working directory. Its source code already exists and passes tests. The prompt`
3. `(the durable source of truth) has CHANGED. Make the MINIMAL code edits needed to realize the`
4. `prompt change — do not restructure or rewrite untouched code, do not reformat unrelated files.`
5. (empty)
6. `Target idiom guidance:`
7. `{adapter.idiom_guidance}`
8. (empty)
9. `Unified diff of the prompt change (old vs new prompt body):`
10. three backticks followed by `diff` (i.e. ```` ```diff ````)
11. `{input.diff.trim()}`
12. a line of three backticks
13. Only if `input.intersecting_entries` is non-empty: (empty), then `The changed prompt lines are currently implemented by these owned code regions.`, then `Prefer editing exactly these regions:`, then one line per entry `- {entry.file} lines {entry.lines.start}-{entry.lines.end}{note}` where `{note}` is the empty string when `entry.note` is empty, otherwise ` — {entry.note}` (space, em dash, space, note).
14. Only if `input.owned_files` is non-empty: (empty), then `Files owned by this module (edit only what the change requires):`, then one `- {file}` per owned file.
15. (empty)
16. `Full current prompt body (the target behavior after the change):`
17. `"""`
18. `{body.trim()}`
19. `"""`
20. (empty)
21. `Requirements:`
22. `- Ensure "{adapter.test_command_label}" passes from this directory before you finish.`
23. `- Update the existing tests to match the new behavior; do not delete unrelated tests.`
24. `- Use only functions/hooks, named exports, and no dead code.`
25. `- Do not edit files under node_modules; use the package manager for dependencies.`
26. Only if `failure` is `Some(f)`: the same **failure block** as `build_agent_task`.

## The reconcile task

`build_reconcile_task(module: &str, prompt_file: &str, body: &str, files: &[ReconcileFile]) -> String`
joins with `\n`:

1. `You are reconciling hand edits back into the prompt (the durable source of truth) for the module "{module}".`
2. `A developer edited generated source files directly, so they no longer match the prompt.`
3. `Amend the prompt file below so that regenerating from it would reproduce the edited behavior.`
4. `Edit ONLY the prompt file(s) named here — NEVER touch the generated source under .napl/src.`
5. (empty)
6. `Prompt file to amend: {prompt_file}`
7. (empty)
8. `Current prompt body:`
9. `"""`
10. `{body.trim()}`
11. `"""`
12. (empty)
13. `Observed source edits (recorded baseline -> current), per file:`
14. For each `ReconcileFile`: `=== FILE: {file.file} ===`, then ```` ```diff ````, then `{file.diff.trim()}`, then a line of three backticks.
15. (empty)
16. `Requirements:`
17. `- Rewrite or extend the prompt prose so its described behavior matches the edited source.`
18. `- Keep the YAML frontmatter valid; change only what the behavior change requires.`
19. `- Do not restate the diff verbatim — describe the intended behavior in the prompt's own voice.`
20. `- Do not edit, create, or delete any file under .napl/src — those are regenerated from the prompt.`

## The change-required escalation

`build_change_required_retry(base_task: &str) -> String` joins with `\n`:

1. `{base_task}`
2. (empty)
3. `CRITICAL: A previous attempt made NO source changes at all, yet the prompt REQUIRES a change.`
4. `Either implement the change now with real edits to the source files, or — only if you are certain`
5. `no code change is needed — state CLEARLY in your final message WHY no change is required (for`
6. `example: the requirement is already satisfied by the existing code, or the instruction cannot be`
7. `acted on because it is unclear or nonsensical). Do not finish silently without doing one of these.`

## The derivation user prompts

`build_ir_derivation_user(module: &str, body: &str, files: &str) -> String` joins
with `\n`: `Module name: {module}`, (empty), `Prompt (the contract, in prose):`,
`"""`, `{body.trim()}`, `"""`, (empty), `Implementing source files:`, `{files}`
(files verbatim, not trimmed).

`build_attribution_user(module: &str, numbered_body: &str, files: &str) -> String`
joins with `\n`: `Module: {module}`, (empty),
`Prompt body (1-based line numbers):`, `{numbered_body}`, (empty),
`Implementing source files (1-based line numbers):`, `{files}`.

`build_attribution_repair(previous_output: &str, error_message: &str) -> String`:
let `previous` be `previous_output.trim()`, then its first 4000 characters, then
trimmed again. Join with `\n`: (empty),
`Your PREVIOUS response was REJECTED as invalid and MUST be corrected. Return ONLY a single`,
then a line whose text is `fenced ` followed by three backticks then `yaml code block containing the list — no prose, no commentary, nothing else. Every`,
`item must reference a "file" that appears EXACTLY as labelled above, and the list must not be`,
`empty. Emit STRICTLY valid YAML with double-quoted string values.`, (empty),
`Your previous invalid response was:`, `"""`, `{previous}`, `"""`, (empty),
`The validation error was:`, `{error_message}`.

`build_ml_derivation_user(module: &str, numbered_body: &str, change_summary: &str, agent_output: &str) -> String`:
let `output` be `last_chars(agent_output.trim(), 4000)`. Join with `\n`:
`Module: {module}`, (empty),
`Prompt body (1-based line numbers) — the durable source of truth:`,
`{numbered_body}`, (empty),
`What the coding agent changed in the source (1-based line numbers), or "NO CHANGES":`,
`{change_summary}`, (empty), `The coding agent's final message:`, `"""`, then
`(no output captured)` if `output` is empty otherwise `{output}`, then `"""`.

## The system-prompt constants

Expose four public `&'static str` constants whose values are **exactly** the text
between the four-backtick fences below (each fence's content is literal — it
contains real triple-backtick sequences and real line breaks; reproduce them
byte-for-byte, with no leading or trailing newline unless shown).

`IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You derive a CONTRACT-LEVEL intermediate representation (IR) from finished source code.
You are given a prompt (the contract in prose) and the source files that implement it.
Produce a YAML document capturing CONTRACTS, not implementation, with these keys:
- module: the exact module name provided.
- deps: list of dependency module names (may be empty).
- types: exported/public types as structural, language-neutral entries, each with a
  "name" and a "description".
- functions: the public functions/components, each with a "name", a language-neutral
  "signature" string, and a "behavior" string covering pre/postconditions.
- tests: the behavioral test cases as data, each with "name", "given", "expect".
Do NOT include control flow, concurrency, memory idioms, or syntax trees.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block and nothing else.
````

`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM`:

````
You map lines of a prompt (the contract, in prose) to the exact lines of generated source
code that implement them. You are given the prompt body with 1-based line numbers, and each
implementing source file with 1-based line numbers.

Produce a YAML list. Each item is a mapping with these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the prompt body.
- file: the source file path exactly as labelled (relative to the target src directory).
- lines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in that file.
- note: a short phrase describing what this code does (e.g. "trims whitespace").

A prompt line may map to multiple code ranges, and one code range may map to multiple
prompt lines — emit one item per concrete mapping. Only map lines that carry real intent;
skip blank lines, headings, and boilerplate. Keep ranges tight.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You are the MACHINE LAYER of a prompt compiler. A prompt (the contract, in prose) was compiled
to source code by a coding agent. Your job is to record — for the human who wrote the prompt —
where the prompt was ambiguous, what you had to assume, and anything else worth surfacing about
this compile.

You are given: the numbered prompt body, the numbered changes the agent made to the source
(or the literal text "NO CHANGES"), and the coding agent's final message.

Output a YAML list (which may be empty). Each item has these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the PROMPT BODY the entry is about.
- kind: one of "ambiguity", "assumption", "note", or "no-op".
- message: a one-line human-facing summary.
- reasoning: a fuller explanation of what was unclear and what was done instead.
- suggestion: OPTIONAL — a proposed clearer rewording of the prompt line(s).

How to choose kind:
- "ambiguity": the prompt line is vague, contradictory, self-referential, out of place, or reads
  like an accidental or nonsensical insertion — an odd literal string, a phrase that does not fit
  the surrounding requirement, or wording a careful engineer would stop and question. BE AGGRESSIVE
  about flagging strange, surprising, or unmotivated phrasing; do NOT smooth it over or assume the
  author must have meant something reasonable.
- "assumption": you had to decide something the prompt left open; record the choice you made.
- "note": something worth surfacing that is neither an ambiguity nor an assumption.
- "no-op": use ONLY when the changes are "NO CHANGES". Explain why nothing was produced — the
  requirement was already satisfied by existing code, or the instruction could not be understood or
  acted upon. When the changes are "NO CHANGES" you MUST emit at least one "no-op" entry.

An empty list is valid ONLY when the prompt is entirely clear AND real changes were made.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`YAML_STRICTNESS` begins with two newline characters and then one line of text —
its exact value is two `\n` characters followed by:
`Emit STRICTLY valid YAML. Quote every string value with double quotes and escape any inner double quotes, especially values containing a colon, quote, or bracket.`
(no trailing newline).

The routing markers matter: `IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain the phrase
`intermediate representation`; `ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain `MACHINE LAYER`;
`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM` must contain neither of those phrases.

`The validation error was:`, `{error_message}`.

`build_ml_derivation_user(module: &str, numbered_body: &str, change_summary: &str, agent_output: &str) -> String`:
let `output` be `last_chars(agent_output.trim(), 4000)`. Join with `\n`:
`Module: {module}`, (empty),
`Prompt body (1-based line numbers) — the durable source of truth:`,
`{numbered_body}`, (empty),
`What the coding agent changed in the source (1-based line numbers), or "NO CHANGES":`,
`{change_summary}`, (empty), `The coding agent's final message:`, `"""`, then
`(no output captured)` if `output` is empty otherwise `{output}`, then `"""`.

## The system-prompt constants

Expose four public `&'static str` constants whose values are **exactly** the text
between the four-backtick fences below (each fence's content is literal — it
contains real triple-backtick sequences and real line breaks; reproduce them
byte-for-byte, with no leading or trailing newline unless shown).

`IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You derive a CONTRACT-LEVEL intermediate representation (IR) from finished source code.
You are given a prompt (the contract in prose) and the source files that implement it.
Produce a YAML document capturing CONTRACTS, not implementation, with these keys:
- module: the exact module name provided.
- deps: list of dependency module names (may be empty).
- types: exported/public types as structural, language-neutral entries, each with a
  "name" and a "description".
- functions: the public functions/components, each with a "name", a language-neutral
  "signature" string, and a "behavior" string covering pre/postconditions.
- tests: the behavioral test cases as data, each with "name", "given", "expect".
Do NOT include control flow, concurrency, memory idioms, or syntax trees.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block and nothing else.
````

`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM`:

````
You map lines of a prompt (the contract, in prose) to the exact lines of generated source
code that implement them. You are given the prompt body with 1-based line numbers, and each
implementing source file with 1-based line numbers.

Produce a YAML list. Each item is a mapping with these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the prompt body.
- file: the source file path exactly as labelled (relative to the target src directory).
- lines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in that file.
- note: a short phrase describing what this code does (e.g. "trims whitespace").

A prompt line may map to multiple code ranges, and one code range may map to multiple
prompt lines — emit one item per concrete mapping. Only map lines that carry real intent;
skip blank lines, headings, and boilerplate. Keep ranges tight.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You are the MACHINE LAYER of a prompt compiler. A prompt (the contract, in prose) was compiled
to source code by a coding agent. Your job is to record — for the human who wrote the prompt —
where the prompt was ambiguous, what you had to assume, and anything else worth surfacing about
this compile.

You are given: the numbered prompt body, the numbered changes the agent made to the source
(or the literal text "NO CHANGES"), and the coding agent's final message.

Output a YAML list (which may be empty). Each item has these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the PROMPT BODY the entry is about.
- kind: one of "ambiguity", "assumption", "note", or "no-op".
- message: a one-line human-facing summary.
- reasoning: a fuller explanation of what was unclear and what was done instead.
- suggestion: OPTIONAL — a proposed clearer rewording of the prompt line(s).

How to choose kind:
- "ambiguity": the prompt line is vague, contradictory, self-referential, out of place, or reads
  like an accidental or nonsensical insertion — an odd literal string, a phrase that does not fit
  the surrounding requirement, or wording a careful engineer would stop and question. BE AGGRESSIVE
  about flagging strange, surprising, or unmotivated phrasing; do NOT smooth it over or assume the
  author must have meant something reasonable.
- "assumption": you had to decide something the prompt left open; record the choice you made.
- "note": something worth surfacing that is neither an ambiguity nor an assumption.
- "no-op": use ONLY when the changes are "NO CHANGES". Explain why nothing was produced — the
  requirement was already satisfied by existing code, or the instruction could not be understood or
  acted upon. When the changes are "NO CHANGES" you MUST emit at least one "no-op" entry.

An empty list is valid ONLY when the prompt is entirely clear AND real changes were made.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`YAML_STRICTNESS` begins with two newline characters and then one line of text —
its exact value is two `\n` characters followed by:
`Emit STRICTLY valid YAML. Quote every string value with double quotes and escape any inner double quotes, especially values containing a colon, quote, or bracket.`
(no trailing newline).

The routing markers matter: `IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain the phrase
`intermediate representation`; `ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain `MACHINE LAYER`;
`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM` must contain neither of those phrases.

mapl.message: No edits made — existing prompts/src/lib.rs already satisfies the full spec.
mapl.reasoning: The change summary is "NO CHANGES". The agent reported the prompt diff was empty (no behavior change) and that the existing prompts crate already implements every builder, constant, and the last_chars helper exactly as specified, with 18 passing tests. Because this is a pure-restatement compile of an already-implemented module, there was nothing to add or edit. No source drift was reported, so the no-op is the correct outcome rather than a missed requirement.

maplContent:
module: prompts
target: rust
entries:
  - promptLines:
      - 1
      - 8
    kind: no-op
    message: No edits made — existing prompts/src/lib.rs already satisfies the full spec.
    reasoning: The change summary is "NO CHANGES". The agent reported the prompt diff was empty (no behavior change) and that the existing prompts crate already implements every builder, constant, and the last_chars helper exactly as specified, with 18 passing tests. Because this is a pure-restatement compile of an already-implemented module, there was nothing to add or edit. No source drift was reported, so the no-op is the correct outcome rather than a missed requirement.

files.prompts.napl:
---
module: prompts
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_frontmatter, schemas_line_range, targets]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: the full-generation task names the module and embeds the spec
    given: { module: greeting, body: "Greet a person by name." }
    expect: { contains: ["implementing the module \"greeting\"", "Greet a person by name."], not_contains: ["INCREMENTAL update"] }
  - name: a prior failure appends the failure block
    given: { failure: "FAIL boom" }
    expect: { contains: ["A previous attempt FAILED its tests.", "FAIL boom"] }
  - name: the incremental task carries the diff and the intersecting region
    given: { region: "greet.ts lines 1-1 note 'builds greeting'" }
    expect: { contains: ["INCREMENTAL update", "-Greet a person by name.", "+Greet a person by name, loudly.", "greet.ts lines 1-1 — builds greeting"] }
  - name: the reconcile task carries the prompt file, body, and per-file diff
    given: { prompt_file: "examples/greeting.napl", file: ".napl/src/typescript/greet.ts" }
    expect: { contains: ["reconciling hand edits", "Prompt file to amend: examples/greeting.napl", "=== FILE: .napl/src/typescript/greet.ts ===", "+HELLO", "NEVER touch the generated source"] }
  - name: the change-required retry appends the critical escalation
    given: { base: "BASE" }
    expect: { starts_with: "BASE", contains: ["CRITICAL: A previous attempt made NO source changes"] }
  - name: the derivation system prompts carry their routing markers
    given: {}
    expect: { ir_contains: "intermediate representation", ml_contains: "MACHINE LAYER", attribution_has_neither: true }
  - name: the machine-layer user prompt marks NO CHANGES and empty output
    given: { change_summary: "NO CHANGES", agent_output: "" }
    expect: { contains: ["NO CHANGES", "(no output captured)"] }
---
# Coding-agent task and derivation-prompt builders

This module builds the exact prompt strings the toolchain feeds to coding agents
and to the IR / attribution / machine-layer derivation LLMs. **The exact text is
load-bearing**: the conformance corpus asserts substrings of these strings, and a
fake backend routes on markers inside the system prompts, so reproduce every
literal byte, line break, and constant precisely. It is pure: no I/O — every
function assembles and returns a `String` (or is a `&'static str` constant).

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `prompts/`: `prompts/Cargo.toml` (package name
`prompts`) and `prompts/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `prompts/`. Ensure
`cargo test` passes from the workspace root before finishing.

## Builds on four modules of this workspace

Each is a sibling member crate generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external
crates.io dependencies), use their public API, and do **not** reimplement them or
depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_frontmatter`** (`../schemas_frontmatter`): `Frontmatter` with public
  fields `module: String`, `deps: Vec<String>`, `targets: Vec<String>`, `tests`.
  The task builders read `module` and `deps`.
- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): `LineRange` with public
  `start`/`end` fields (`u32`).
- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): `AttributionEntry`
  (`Clone`) with public `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`,
  `lines: LineRange`, `note: String`.
- **`targets`** (`../targets`): `TargetAdapter` with a public
  `idiom_guidance: String` and a public `test_command_label: &'static str`, and
  the function `get_adapter(name: &str) -> Result<TargetAdapter, String>`. The
  task builders read `idiom_guidance` and `test_command_label` from the adapter
  they are given.

## The types

Expose these public types:

- `DepSummary` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`): public `module: String` and
  `summary: String`.
- `IncrementalTaskInput` (`Debug`, `Clone`): public `module: String`,
  `diff: String`, `intersecting_entries: Vec<AttributionEntry>`, and
  `owned_files: Vec<String>`.
- `ReconcileFile` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`): public `file: String` and
  `diff: String`.

## A shared truncation helper

`last_chars(text, count)` returns the last `count` **characters** (Unicode
scalar values, not bytes) of `text`; if `text` has `count` or fewer characters it
returns the whole `text`. Used to cap failure output at 6000 and agent output at
4000 characters below.

## The full-generation task

`build_agent_task(adapter: &TargetAdapter, frontmatter: &Frontmatter, body: &str, deps: &[DepSummary], failure: Option<&str>) -> String`
joins the following lines with `\n` (a bare bullet like `(empty)` denotes an
empty line):

1. `You are a coding agent implementing the module "{frontmatter.module}" as real, runnable`
2. `source code in the current working directory. Write and edit files directly; scaffold the`
3. `project and install dependencies as needed. The prompt below is the durable source of truth.`
4. (empty)
5. `Target idiom guidance:`
6. `{adapter.idiom_guidance}`
7. (empty)
8. `Module: {frontmatter.module}`
9. Only if `frontmatter.deps` is non-empty: `Declared dependencies: {frontmatter.deps joined by ", "}`
10. Only if `deps` is non-empty, three-plus lines: (empty), then `Other modules in this project (for context, do not reimplement them):`, then one `- {dep.module}: {dep.summary}` per `DepSummary`.
11. (empty)
12. `Specification to implement (implement exactly this behavior):`
13. `"""`
14. `{body.trim()}`
15. `"""`
16. (empty)
17. `Requirements:`
18. `- Ensure "{adapter.test_command_label}" passes from this directory before you finish.`
19. `- Write a real test suite covering the described behavior.`
20. `- Use only functions/hooks, named exports, and no dead code.`
21. `- Do not edit files under node_modules; use the package manager for dependencies.`
22. Only if `failure` is `Some(f)`, the **failure block**: (empty), then `A previous attempt FAILED its tests. Read the current files, fix the code and/or tests,`, then `and make the suite pass. Test output from the failed attempt:`, then a line of three backticks, then `{last_chars(f.trim(), 6000)}`, then a line of three backticks.

## The incremental task

`build_incremental_task(adapter: &TargetAdapter, frontmatter: &Frontmatter, body: &str, input: &IncrementalTaskInput, failure: Option<&str>) -> String`
joins with `\n`:

1. `You are a coding agent making an INCREMENTAL update to the existing module "{frontmatter.module}"`
2. `in the current working directory. Its source code already exists and passes tests. The prompt`
3. `(the durable source of truth) has CHANGED. Make the MINIMAL code edits needed to realize the`
4. `prompt change — do not restructure or rewrite untouched code, do not reformat unrelated files.`
5. (empty)
6. `Target idiom guidance:`
7. `{adapter.idiom_guidance}`
8. (empty)
9. `Unified diff of the prompt change (old vs new prompt body):`
10. three backticks followed by `diff` (i.e. ```` ```diff ````)
11. `{input.diff.trim()}`
12. a line of three backticks
13. Only if `input.intersecting_entries` is non-empty: (empty), then `The changed prompt lines are currently implemented by these owned code regions.`, then `Prefer editing exactly these regions:`, then one line per entry `- {entry.file} lines {entry.lines.start}-{entry.lines.end}{note}` where `{note}` is the empty string when `entry.note` is empty, otherwise ` — {entry.note}` (space, em dash, space, note).
14. Only if `input.owned_files` is non-empty: (empty), then `Files owned by this module (edit only what the change requires):`, then one `- {file}` per owned file.
15. (empty)
16. `Full current prompt body (the target behavior after the change):`
17. `"""`
18. `{body.trim()}`
19. `"""`
20. (empty)
21. `Requirements:`
22. `- Ensure "{adapter.test_command_label}" passes from this directory before you finish.`
23. `- Update the existing tests to match the new behavior; do not delete unrelated tests.`
24. `- Use only functions/hooks, named exports, and no dead code.`
25. `- Do not edit files under node_modules; use the package manager for dependencies.`
26. Only if `failure` is `Some(f)`: the same **failure block** as `build_agent_task`.

## The reconcile task

`build_reconcile_task(module: &str, prompt_file: &str, body: &str, files: &[ReconcileFile]) -> String`
joins with `\n`:

1. `You are reconciling hand edits back into the prompt (the durable source of truth) for the module "{module}".`
2. `A developer edited generated source files directly, so they no longer match the prompt.`
3. `Amend the prompt file below so that regenerating from it would reproduce the edited behavior.`
4. `Edit ONLY the prompt file(s) named here — NEVER touch the generated source under .napl/src.`
5. (empty)
6. `Prompt file to amend: {prompt_file}`
7. (empty)
8. `Current prompt body:`
9. `"""`
10. `{body.trim()}`
11. `"""`
12. (empty)
13. `Observed source edits (recorded baseline -> current), per file:`
14. For each `ReconcileFile`: `=== FILE: {file.file} ===`, then ```` ```diff ````, then `{file.diff.trim()}`, then a line of three backticks.
15. (empty)
16. `Requirements:`
17. `- Rewrite or extend the prompt prose so its described behavior matches the edited source.`
18. `- Keep the YAML frontmatter valid; change only what the behavior change requires.`
19. `- Do not restate the diff verbatim — describe the intended behavior in the prompt's own voice.`
20. `- Do not edit, create, or delete any file under .napl/src — those are regenerated from the prompt.`

## The change-required escalation

`build_change_required_retry(base_task: &str) -> String` joins with `\n`:

1. `{base_task}`
2. (empty)
3. `CRITICAL: A previous attempt made NO source changes at all, yet the prompt REQUIRES a change.`
4. `Either implement the change now with real edits to the source files, or — only if you are certain`
5. `no code change is needed — state CLEARLY in your final message WHY no change is required (for`
6. `example: the requirement is already satisfied by the existing code, or the instruction cannot be`
7. `acted on because it is unclear or nonsensical). Do not finish silently without doing one of these.`

## The derivation user prompts

`build_ir_derivation_user(module: &str, body: &str, files: &str) -> String` joins
with `\n`: `Module name: {module}`, (empty), `Prompt (the contract, in prose):`,
`"""`, `{body.trim()}`, `"""`, (empty), `Implementing source files:`, `{files}`
(files verbatim, not trimmed).

`build_attribution_user(module: &str, numbered_body: &str, files: &str) -> String`
joins with `\n`: `Module: {module}`, (empty),
`Prompt body (1-based line numbers):`, `{numbered_body}`, (empty),
`Implementing source files (1-based line numbers):`, `{files}`.

`build_attribution_repair(previous_output: &str, error_message: &str) -> String`:
let `previous` be `previous_output.trim()`, then its first 4000 characters, then
trimmed again. Join with `\n`: (empty),
`Your PREVIOUS response was REJECTED as invalid and MUST be corrected. Return ONLY a single`,
then a line whose text is `fenced ` followed by three backticks then `yaml code block containing the list — no prose, no commentary, nothing else. Every`,
`item must reference a "file" that appears EXACTLY as labelled above, and the list must not be`,
`empty. Emit STRICTLY valid YAML with double-quoted string values.`, (empty),
`Your previous invalid response was:`, `"""`, `{previous}`, `"""`, (empty),
`The validation error was:`, `{error_message}`.

`build_ml_derivation_user(module: &str, numbered_body: &str, change_summary: &str, agent_output: &str) -> String`:
let `output` be `last_chars(agent_output.trim(), 4000)`. Join with `\n`:
`Module: {module}`, (empty),
`Prompt body (1-based line numbers) — the durable source of truth:`,
`{numbered_body}`, (empty),
`What the coding agent changed in the source (1-based line numbers), or "NO CHANGES":`,
`{change_summary}`, (empty), `The coding agent's final message:`, `"""`, then
`(no output captured)` if `output` is empty otherwise `{output}`, then `"""`.

## The system-prompt constants

Expose four public `&'static str` constants whose values are **exactly** the text
between the four-backtick fences below (each fence's content is literal — it
contains real triple-backtick sequences and real line breaks; reproduce them
byte-for-byte, with no leading or trailing newline unless shown).

`IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You derive a CONTRACT-LEVEL intermediate representation (IR) from finished source code.
You are given a prompt (the contract in prose) and the source files that implement it.
Produce a YAML document capturing CONTRACTS, not implementation, with these keys:
- module: the exact module name provided.
- deps: list of dependency module names (may be empty).
- types: exported/public types as structural, language-neutral entries, each with a
  "name" and a "description".
- functions: the public functions/components, each with a "name", a language-neutral
  "signature" string, and a "behavior" string covering pre/postconditions.
- tests: the behavioral test cases as data, each with "name", "given", "expect".
Do NOT include control flow, concurrency, memory idioms, or syntax trees.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block and nothing else.
````

`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM`:

````
You map lines of a prompt (the contract, in prose) to the exact lines of generated source
code that implement them. You are given the prompt body with 1-based line numbers, and each
implementing source file with 1-based line numbers.

Produce a YAML list. Each item is a mapping with these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the prompt body.
- file: the source file path exactly as labelled (relative to the target src directory).
- lines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in that file.
- note: a short phrase describing what this code does (e.g. "trims whitespace").

A prompt line may map to multiple code ranges, and one code range may map to multiple
prompt lines — emit one item per concrete mapping. Only map lines that carry real intent;
skip blank lines, headings, and boilerplate. Keep ranges tight.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM`:

````
You are the MACHINE LAYER of a prompt compiler. A prompt (the contract, in prose) was compiled
to source code by a coding agent. Your job is to record — for the human who wrote the prompt —
where the prompt was ambiguous, what you had to assume, and anything else worth surfacing about
this compile.

You are given: the numbered prompt body, the numbered changes the agent made to the source
(or the literal text "NO CHANGES"), and the coding agent's final message.

Output a YAML list (which may be empty). Each item has these keys:
- promptLines: [start, end]  — 1-based inclusive line range in the PROMPT BODY the entry is about.
- kind: one of "ambiguity", "assumption", "note", or "no-op".
- message: a one-line human-facing summary.
- reasoning: a fuller explanation of what was unclear and what was done instead.
- suggestion: OPTIONAL — a proposed clearer rewording of the prompt line(s).

How to choose kind:
- "ambiguity": the prompt line is vague, contradictory, self-referential, out of place, or reads
  like an accidental or nonsensical insertion — an odd literal string, a phrase that does not fit
  the surrounding requirement, or wording a careful engineer would stop and question. BE AGGRESSIVE
  about flagging strange, surprising, or unmotivated phrasing; do NOT smooth it over or assume the
  author must have meant something reasonable.
- "assumption": you had to decide something the prompt left open; record the choice you made.
- "note": something worth surfacing that is neither an ambiguity nor an assumption.
- "no-op": use ONLY when the changes are "NO CHANGES". Explain why nothing was produced — the
  requirement was already satisfied by existing code, or the instruction could not be understood or
  acted upon. When the changes are "NO CHANGES" you MUST emit at least one "no-op" entry.

An empty list is valid ONLY when the prompt is entirely clear AND real changes were made.
Output ONLY a single fenced ```yaml code block containing the list, and nothing else.
````

`YAML_STRICTNESS` begins with two newline characters and then one line of text —
its exact value is two `\n` characters followed by:
`Emit STRICTLY valid YAML. Quote every string value with double quotes and escape any inner double quotes, especially values containing a colon, quote, or bracket.`
(no trailing newline).

The routing markers matter: `IR_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain the phrase
`intermediate representation`; `ML_DERIVATION_SYSTEM` must contain `MACHINE LAYER`;
`ATTRIBUTION_SYSTEM` must contain neither of those phrases.

entries.note: last_chars returns last count chars by scalar value
DepSummary struct with module and summary
failure block builder with 6000 char cap
ml derivation signature and last_chars output

entry.message: No edits made — existing prompts/src/lib.rs already satisfies the full spec.
entry.reasoning: The change summary is "NO CHANGES". The agent reported the prompt diff was empty (no behavior change) and that the existing prompts crate already implements every builder, constant, and the last_chars helper exactly as specified, with 18 passing tests. Because this is a pure-restatement compile of an already-implemented module, there was nothing to add or edit. No source drift was reported, so the no-op is the correct outcome rather than a missed requirement.
